title: Drake Maye surprises fan wearing ‘I [Heart] Drake Maye’ t-shirt at Bruins game
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Drake Maye and Ann Michael Maye attended the Bruins game against the Dallas Stars at TD Garden on Tuesday.
A fan wearing an 'I [Heart] Drake Maye' t-shirt was acknowledged by Maye during the Bruins' 6-3 win.
Earlier, Drake Maye participated in a 'Saving by Shaving' event in Quincy benefiting Boston Children's Hospital, raising over $4 million.

title: Former Celtics coach Doc Rivers reportedly voted into Basketball Hall of Fame
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Doc Rivers will be inducted into the Naismith Memorial Basketball Hall of Fame as part of the Class of 2026.
Rivers led the Boston Celtics to their 17th NBA championship in the 2007-08 season.
Rivers, with a 1,191-861 coaching record, has won over 1,000 career games across 27 NBA seasons.

title: Drake Maye shaves his head as part of charity event benefiting Boston Children’s Hospital
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Drake Maye shaved his head for Granite Telecommunications' charity event, raising $4.15 million for Boston Children's Hospital.
Granite Telecommunications donated $2,500 per participant, with CEO Rob Hale matching donations, aiding the fundraising total.
Maye launched 'The MayeDay Family Foundation' to assist children and families in New England and North Carolina.

title: Boston police officer criminally charged after allegedly leaving loaded gun unsecured in vehicle
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Officer Sean Cullen charged with firearms storage violations after an incident in West Roxbury.
Charges include a felony for improper storage of a large capacity firearm and a misdemeanor.
Cullen was placed on administrative leave and his law enforcement certification was suspended.

title: N.H. governor’s security aid rescues Mass. driver from fiery toll booth crash
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: A Massachusetts driver was seriously injured after crashing into the Bedford Toll Plaza on the Everett Turnpike and catching fire.
N.H. Gov. Kelly Ayotte and her security detail aided in rescuing the trapped driver from the flaming vehicle.
The crash is under investigation, with portions of the Everett Turnpike closed for police inquiries; no charges filed against the driver.

title: Mike Vrabel talks A.J. Brown, Patriots draft, and other takeaways from NFL owners’ meetings
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots' coach Mike Vrabel discussed potential trades for A.J. Brown at NFL owners' meetings in Phoenix.
Vrabel emphasized the need for QB Drake Maye to improve his game control as the Patriots' key player.
The Patriots plan to prioritize the best player available in the NFL Draft, avoiding drafting solely based on need.

title: Mass. man charged with murdering wife, allegedly told 911 operator ‘my gun went off’
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Mark Opper, 56, charged with murdering his wife, Diane Opper, in their Belchertown home on March 27.
Opper allegedly told a 911 operator his gun went off, police found multiple gunshot wounds on Diane Opper.
Police recovered multiple guns; Opper is held without bail, scheduled for a court hearing on April 27.

title: Brockton man facing 14 charges after allegedly engaging in shootout with police
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Jailson Andrade, 44, was arraigned on 14 charges after a shootout with Brockton police following a chase.
Andrade is accused of assault to murder, assault with a dangerous weapon, and carrying an unlicensed firearm.
A nearby 71-year-old man and Andrade sustained non-life-threatening injuries during the shootout and were hospitalized.

title: 1 killed, 1 injured in Charlton crash, driver reportedly refused to stop for police
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: A fatal crash occurred on Route 20 in Charlton, involving a speeding pick-up truck and an SUV.
One driver was killed and another was airlifted to UMass Memorial Hospital with non-life-threatening injuries.
The crash followed a refusal to stop by a speeding driver; police and state detectives are investigating.

title: Victim identified in fatal Roxbury hit-and-run
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Steven Stewart, 47, from Worcester, was identified as the victim of a fatal Roxbury hit-and-run on March 20.
Stewart was struck near Southampton and Bradston Streets at 11:34 a.m. and later pronounced dead at a hospital.
The suspect fled the scene, and while the vehicle was found, no arrests have been made; investigation continues.
